lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm really just looking for a quest...

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: Fortune favours us both then adventurer. I suggest you go and speak to King Arthur.

sections.title: After talking to Sir Gawain and learning about Keep Le Faye

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea how to break into Mordred's fort do you?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: I am afraid not. Would that we could! Mordred and his cronies have been thorns in our side for far too long already!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok. Thanks. See you later!

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: Take care adventurer, Mordred is an evil and powerful foe.

sections.title: After Morgan Le Faye tells you how to release Merlin

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Know anything about Excalibur?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: Um... it's a really good sword?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Know where I can find it?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: Nope, sorry.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Merlin's in a crystal. Little help?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: That is what we were hoping for from you, adventurer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. Well, ok, thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: All Knights of the Round thank you for your assistance in this trying time for us.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: You are looking for the Grail now adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: The best of luck to you! Make the name of Camelot proud, and bring it back to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If we break out of this cell, do you reckon you can take on the Black Knights in the keep?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: You just let me at them. We'll see how they like surprise attacks.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: How are you going to get all of us out of here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure yet. Have you got any useful objects in your pockets?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: After getting things from his pockets

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: How are you going to get all of us out of here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure yet. Have you got any useful objects in your pockets?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: I already gave you everything I had in my pockets.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: Let me out! Let me out, I say!

sections.title: While the Knights of the Round Table fight the black knights in Keep Le Faye

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Give me an R. Give me an O. Give me a U..

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: In the amount of time it will take you to spell out Round Table Knights, you could have found the Grail.

sections.title: Using the Holy grail on him

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: What am I supposed to do with that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you let a bunch of Black Knights get the better of you? I thought you guys were the toughest knights around.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: We are true knights who fight for honour. Those Black Knights didn't fight fair, so we were unprepared. Most unchivalrous of them.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: Thank you for your assistance in our time of need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're welcome.
